pages.full_text: Eve at a temple on the Long Walk, Farnborough Hall.
photo_captions: Eve at a temple on the Long Walk, Farnborough Hall.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a glued-in colour photograph of a circular domed temple/folly with a person (Eve) standing in front of it. The handwritten caption is below the photo. No handwritten page number visible.

pages.full_text:
__Saturday June 1st.__
	1pm we - P,J,C, S,E, set off for Laurel Spooner's 40th birthday party & Laurel & Dick's 20th Wedding Anniversary; at Layer Marney Tower near Colchester, and old Tudor house. The children were supposed to be ghouls - John was a policeman, Charles a cowboy. Simon had made John 'police' badges on the new computer.
	We are camping on the island of Mersea, Ten Farm campsite. **** Clean loos, very restful, glorious views [illegible] [illegible] of the sea with sail boats. Sand & gorse like Thorpeness. We got to bed at 12.30. & woke at 8am.
Sunday After breakfast we went for a walk along the shingle sand banks. Mud flats, tide coming in, hundreds of shells. We had lunch in Mersea at a good French restaurant; the Blackwater, with Matt & Jane  Very enjoyable. We set off for home.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '4' visible in top-right corner. 'Saturday June 1st.' and 'Mersea, Ten Farm campsite.' appear underlined. The stars '****' appear to be a rating for the campsite. Two words after 'glorious views' are unclear — possibly 'A A' (could be AA rating symbols or decorative marks) followed by 'of the'; marked as [illegible]. 'Mud flats' read as 'Mud flats' though it could be 'Mudflats'. Year cannot be confirmed from the page itself — 'Saturday June 1st' falls on a Saturday in various years; within volume A range (1989–2003) candidates include 1991 and 1996 and 2002; no year is written on the page.
